about.title: About {siteName}

faq.q1: How can I update my business hours?
faq.a1: Go to Business in your dashboard, then Services tab. You can edit opening hours for each day there.
faq.q2: How can I reply to reviews?
faq.a2: Go to My Reviews in your dashboard, open a review, then click Reply to publish your public response.
faq.q3: What is the difference between Growth and Gold?
faq.a3: Growth supports one business with core visibility tools. Gold supports up to 5 businesses with advanced analytics and communication features.

analyze.title: Analyze Job Offer
analyze.description: Paste a job offer text or URL and receive an explainable market analysis.
analyze.loginRequired: You need to be logged in to run and save job offer analyses in this module.

history.title: My Job Offer Analyses
history.description: Review previously saved job offer analyses.

loginRequired.title: Login required
loginRequired.description: You need an account to access saved analyses.

history.subtitle: Saved outputs from the standalone job-offers module.

empty.title: No analyses yet
empty.description: Run your first offer analysis to start building a private history.

filters.noMatch: No analyses match your current filters.

detail.title: Job Offer Analysis Detail
detail.description: View a saved job offer analysis.

form.decodeTitle: Decode a job offer before you commit
form.decodeDesc: AI extracts the offer, checks market signals, and turns the result into a private multi-step decision workspace.
form.provideOfferDesc: Paste the announcement text or switch to a public link. After analysis, the result opens on its own page.
form.pastePlaceholder: Paste a LinkedIn, Rekrute, Indeed, or Emploi.ma job post here...
form.pasteDesc: Best for copied job ads, screenshots transcribed to text, or incomplete posts you still want to pressure-test.
form.linkDesc: Works when the source page is public and readable. If extraction is partial, AI still returns useful follow-up questions.
form.loginToSave: Login required to save and compare results
form.incompleteNote: Some offers are incomplete. AI will still surface useful questions, confidence levels, and the best next action instead of pretending the evidence is stronger than it is.

loadingStep2.title: Step 2 of 5: AI is reading the source
loadingStep2.body: We are extracting the visible role, company, salary, and work conditions from the offer.

loadingStep2Alt.title: Step 2 of 5: Structuring what is clear
loadingStep2Alt.body: AI is identifying missing fields, confidence levels, and what still needs verification.

loadingStep3.title: Step 3 of 5: Comparing with market and employer signals
loadingStep3.body: We are checking benchmarks, employer mapping, and any similar offer context available.

loadingStep3Alt.title: Step 3 of 5: Building your decision workspace
loadingStep3Alt.body: Your verdict, next actions, recruiter questions, and context blocks are being prepared.

result.decisionSupportNote: AI helps extract and structure the offer, then combines it with market and employer context. This is decision support, not ground truth.
result.footerNote: Keep this analysis private, compare it with alternatives, and come back after recruiter follow-up.

offer.label: Offer
offer.title: Read the offer itself
offer.body: Verdict, facts, confidence, and what is still missing.

employer.label: Employer
employer.title: Check employer context
employer.body: Business signal, review context, and mapped employer data.

compare.label: Compare
compare.title: Compare nearby alternatives
compare.body: See how this role stacks up against similar analyzed offers.

actions.label: Next actions
actions.title: Decide what to do next
actions.body: Recruiter questions, dynamic CTAs, and follow-up actions.

details.label: Details
details.title: Inspect diagnostics
details.body: Source details, detected fields, and confidence diagnostics.

fallbacks.positive: The offer includes at least a few structured details instead of being completely opaque.
fallbacks.concern: There is no major red flag here, but several decision-critical details still need confirmation.
fallbacks.missing: Most core fields are visible, but package and reporting details should still be verified.

presentation.salaryMissingNote: Compensation was not visible in the source.
presentation.salaryMentionedNote: Needs verification before you treat this range as firm.
presentation.salaryVisibleNote: Visible enough to use in the current market read.

high.label: High confidence
high.note: Salary and market context are solid enough for a firm diagnosis.

medium.label: Medium confidence
medium.note: There is context, but key details should still be verified before deciding.

low.label: Low confidence
low.note: Market coverage is low, read as an indication rather than a verdict.
low.noteNoSalary: Salary is missing, analysis cannot give a firm market verdict.

incomplete.title: Useful signal, incomplete verdict
incomplete.titleNoSalary: Too incomplete for a pay verdict
incomplete.summary: Some parts of the offer are readable, but the evidence is not strong enough for a reliable market conclusion yet.
incomplete.summaryNoSalary: This offer can still help you prepare smarter questions, but salary is missing so it should not be treated as fair, strong, or weak pay.

strong.title: This offer reads as unusually competitive
strong.summary: The pay signal and structure both look stronger than a typical vague listing, but the final decision should still depend on scope, manager quality, and growth path.

above.title: This offer looks better than the local baseline
above.summary: The offer shows enough evidence to suggest a favorable read, but you should still verify the exact scope, reporting line, and package conditions.

fair.title: This offer looks roughly in line with the market
fair.summary: Nothing here looks dramatically off-market, but that does not automatically make it a good personal decision.

weak.title: This offer looks weaker than the visible benchmark
weak.summary: The available evidence suggests a less attractive read than the market baseline. Clarify the package before spending too much time.

negotiation.title: Use market data to negotiate
negotiation.description: Based on market benchmarks for this role. Customize before sending.

outcome.question: What did you decide?
outcome.description: Your anonymous answer helps calibrate offers for everyone in Morocco.
outcome.thanksNote: Thank you — this helps build real data for Morocco.

fallbacks.positive: The offer has at least enough structure to support a useful review.
fallbacks.concern: No major concern stands out yet, but key details still need verification.

diagnostics.title: Facts vs diagnostics
diagnostics.trigger: Expand detected fields and source details

comparison.title: How this compares to similar offers
comparison.noOffers: No strong comparable offers are visible yet for this role and market combination. When more approved analyses accumulate, this section becomes comparison evidence rather than guesswork.

gaps.allVisible: The main decision-critical fields are visible. Use the follow-up actions to confirm package details and manager fit.

salary.title: Salary band missing
salary.insight: The employer is asking for your time before disclosing the most basic compensation anchor.
salary.action: Ask for the salary band before committing to multiple interview rounds.

contract.title: Contract type unclear
contract.insight: Without contract clarity, stability and legal framing are still open questions.
contract.action: Ask whether this is CDI, CDD, freelance, or internship.

workModel.title: Work model not stated
workModel.insight: On-site, hybrid, and remote expectations shape commute cost and quality of life.
workModel.action: Clarify office presence, flexibility, and any travel expectations.

benefits.title: Benefits package unclear
benefits.insight: An offer can look fine on base salary while still being weak on the global package.
benefits.action: Ask for the full package, not just base salary.

seniority.title: Leveling is vague
seniority.insight: If level is not explicit, the employer may still be flexible on expectations or budget.
seniority.action: Ask what level they have budgeted and what success looks like in 90 days.

clarity.title: Clarity
clarity.note: How much concrete structure the offer gives you up front.

compensation.title: Compensation visibility
compensation.note: Salary is visible, but market judgment still depends on benchmark strength.
compensation.noteHidden: No salary was disclosed, so pay quality should not be assumed.

conditions.title: Work conditions visibility
conditions.note: Contract, work model, city, and benefits shape day-to-day reality.

value.title: Career value
value.note: A proxy for role quality, package depth, and growth visibility.

benchmark.title: Benchmark confidence
benchmark.note: Strength of the database used for comparison.

risk.title: Risk level
risk.note: Higher means more ambiguity, downside, or negotiation risk.

ask_salary.title: Ask for salary before continuing
ask_salary.body: Compensation details are missing or too unclear to justify a long process yet.

review_employer.title: Review employer before applying
review_employer.body: The employer is mapped, but public sentiment is mixed enough that you should review context.

compare_offers.title: Compare with similar offers
compare_offers.body: You have enough matching data to compare this against nearby alternatives.

check_benchmarks.title: Check salary benchmarks
check_benchmarks.body: Use the role and city benchmark page to pressure-test this offer.

view_company.title: View company reviews
view_company.body: This offer is mapped confidently enough to add employer context from reviews.

view_company_rail.title: View company reviews
view_company_rail.body: Use employee reviews to add context to the offer without distorting the salary verdict.

open_company_page.title: Open company page
open_company_page.body: See the public company profile, business details, and broader hiring context.

open_company_salary.title: Check company salary context
open_company_salary.body: Use company salary context to judge whether this offer fits the broader pattern.

use_questions.title: Use recruiter questions next
use_questions.body: Turn the analysis into a sharper recruiter conversation before committing more time.

positives.salaryDisclosed: Salary is disclosed, which saves you from guessing basic compensation terms.
positives.contractStated: The contract type is stated as {type}.
positives.workModelStated: The work model is explicitly stated as {model}.
positives.cityStated: The offer is tied to a specific market: {city}.

concerns.salaryMissing: Salary is missing, so the offer should not be framed as fair or strong pay.
concerns.wideRange: The salary range is wide, which can hide level mismatch.
concerns.missingLocation: Location is unclear, which makes commute and market fit harder to judge.
concerns.missingContract: Contract type is missing, so employment stability is still unclear.
concerns.missingWorkModel: Work model is not stated, which usually becomes a late-stage friction point.
concerns.missingSeniority: Seniority is unclear, which can mask both workload and salary expectations.
concerns.lowConfidence: Benchmark confidence is low, so this read should be used as directional guidance only.
concerns.lowCompanyConfidence: Company identification may need manual confirmation.
concerns.lowTitleConfidence: The role title may be loosely extracted, so scope should be clarified early.
concerns.noBenefits: Benefits are not visible, so total package quality is still hard to judge.

hidden.noSalaryLeverage: Omitting salary usually shifts leverage toward later interview stages.
hidden.wideRangeLeverage: A wide salary range often means the role level is still loose.
hidden.vagueConditions: When basic work conditions are vague, the hiring brief itself may still be under-defined.
hidden.verifyCompany: The company name may not be cleanly stated, so verify who is really hiring.
hidden.clarificationOnly: This read is more useful for clarification than for final judgment.

mixedSentiment.title: Compensation is unclear and employee sentiment is mixed
mixedSentiment.body: The offer is missing salary details, and the employer signal is not strong enough to offset that.

weakReputation.title: Offer looks fine, but employer reputation is weak
weakReputation.body: The visible offer may still be usable, but public sentiment is weak enough that you should be careful.

positiveReinforcement.title: Employer reviews reinforce a positive read
positiveReinforcement.body: The offer signal and the employer context point in the same direction.

questions.cityRelocation: Where is the role based, and is relocation or regular travel expected?
questions.packageStructure: How is the package structured between fixed pay, variable pay, bonuses, and benefits?
questions.reportingTeam: Who would this role report to, and how is the team currently structured?
questions.performanceSuccess: What would strong performance look like in the first 3 to 6 months?

topics.roleScope: Role scope, ownership, and first-quarter expectations
topics.teamStructure: Team structure, reporting line, and decision-making cadence
topics.compensationVague: Compensation expectations and how the package is framed
topics.packageDetails: Package details, variable pay, and evaluation criteria
topics.workModelVague: Work model, office rhythm, and schedule flexibility
topics.remoteStyle: Remote collaboration style, availability expectations, and tooling
topics.hybridRhythm: Hybrid rhythm, on-site expectations, and cross-team coordination
topics.onsiteCommute: On-site expectations, commute practicality, and manager access
topics.growthPath: Growth path, promotion logic, and stretch opportunities

employerSignal.strong: This employer has a relatively strong public signal (reviews, verification).
employerSignal.mixed: This employer has some usable public context, but the signal is not definitive.
employerSignal.limited: Public employer context is still thin, treat as additional context.

nextStep.askSalary: Ask for the salary band before investing more time.
nextStep.lowConfidence: Use this as a question-prep tool, not a final decision.
nextStep.belowMarket: Only continue if the role scope or growth path clearly compensates for the weaker pay signal.
nextStep.strongOffer: Worth a deeper conversation, but verify manager quality.
nextStep.baseline: Treat this as a decent baseline and pressure-test the missing conditions.
